domain:
In an e-commerce retail organization, customers are registered with unique ids to place orders that includes multiple products. An order also has a unique identifier and consists of one or more products, which are described by their name and description. When the customer completes the order, it is necessary to specify the quantity requested and the price for the item is updated.

The retailer also ask customers to provide feedback by writing reviews for specific products. These reviews capture the opinion of a customer regarding a product, including the date it was written and a rating score. Finally, the marketing department organizes promotional campaigns. A product may be included in various promotions over time. Whenever a product is part of a campaign, specific terms are established for that offer, such as the applicable discount rate and a validity period defined by a start and end date.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Customer in the relationship with Order?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an order can exist without any associated customer.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an order must have at least one associated customer.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Order can exist without Customer.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Order must have at least one Customer.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Order must have at least one Customer.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of Customer in the relationship with Order?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, an order is associated with exactly one customer.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, an order can be associated with multiple customers.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each order links to one customer.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each order can link to multiple customers.
questions.selected: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each order links to one customer.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Order in the relationship with Customer?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a customer can exist without any associated order.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a customer must have at least one associated order.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Customer can exist without Order.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Customer must have at least one Order.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Customer can exist without Order.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Customer can exist without Order.  

This option is better because the domain description does not specify that a customer must have placed an order to exist; it only states that customers are registered to place orders, implying that they can exist independently of any orders.

questions.question:

1. Maximum cardinality of one, a customer is associated with exactly one order.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a customer can be associated with multiple orders.

questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each customer links to one order.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each customer can link to multiple orders.
questions.selected: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each customer links to one order.

questions.question:
Should promotion be modeled as a separate class associated with ProductPromotion, or as a simple representation within ProductPromotion?

For example:
1. Promotion is a separate class with attributes: . This allows users to query and filter by individual promotion properties.
2. Promotion is modeled as a simple represntation in ProductPromotion. This is suitable when promotion details are not needed.
Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The Promotion is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The promotion is a simple representation in ProductPromotion.
questions.selected: Option 1: The Promotion is a separate class with its own attributes.

questions.question:
Should OrderItem be modeled as an association class linking Product and Order, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: OrderItem exists only as part of the relationship between Product and Order. A order item cannot exist without the link between Product and Order.
2. Regular class: OrderItem is a class that has links to Product and Order. In this case multiple order items can exist for every Product and Order.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, OrderItem as association class linking Product and Order.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, OrderItem as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, OrderItem as association class linking Product and Order.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 1: Association class, OrderItem as association class linking Product and Order.  

This option aligns with the domain description, as it indicates that an order item is inherently tied to both a product and an order, and cannot exist independently of this relationship.

questions.question:
Should Review be modeled as an association class linking Customer and Product, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: Review exists only as part of the relationship between Customer and Product. A review cannot exist without the link between Customer and Product.
2. Regular class: Review is a class that has links to Customer and Product. In this case multiple reviews can exist for every Customer and Product.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, Review as association class linking Customer and Product.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, Review as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, Review as association class linking Customer and Product.

questions.question:
Should ProductPromotion be modeled as an association class linking Product and Promotion, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: ProductPromotion exists only as part of the relationship between Product and Promotion. A product promotion cannot exist without the link between Product and Promotion.
2. Regular class: ProductPromotion is a class that has links to Product and Promotion. In this case multiple product promotions can exist for every Product and Promotion.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, ProductPromotion as association class linking Product and Promotion.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, ProductPromotion as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 2: Regular class, ProductPromotion as separate class with two associations.
